title: Patbingsu Red Bean Shaved Ice

seo.title: Authentic Patbingsu Recipe: Korean Red Bean Shaved Ice
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Korean Patbingsu with this easy recipe! Enjoy a refreshing red bean shaved ice dessert, perfect for summer. Get the best Patbingsu recipe here!
lowCompetitionKeywords: Korean red bean shaved ice dessert
seo.h1: Authentic Patbingsu Recipe: Korean Red Bean Shaved Ice
h2Headings: How to Make Patbingsu: Step-by-Step Guide
seo.imageAlt: A bowl of authentic Patbingsu, Korean red bean shaved ice, topped with sweet red beans, tteok, and condensed milk.
seo.imageCaption: Cool down with a delicious bowl of homemade Patbingsu, a classic Korean red bean shaved ice dessert!

description: Patbingsu is a beloved Korean dessert that offers a refreshing escape from the heat, featuring finely shaved ice as its base. This delightful treat is traditionally adorned with a generous serving of sweet red bean paste and a drizzle of condensed milk. It's a perfect balance of creamy, sweet, and icy textures, making it an irresistible summer indulgence.

ingredients.item: Sweet red bean paste (canned or homemade)
ingredients.notes: or make your own from dried red beans

instructions.title: Prepare the Sweet Red Bean Paste
instructions.text: If using dried red beans, rinse 1 cup of beans and soak overnight. Drain, then boil in fresh water for 10 minutes, drain again. Cover with 3 cups of fresh water and simmer for 30-40 minutes until tender. Drain most water, mash beans, then stir in 1/2 cup sugar and a pinch of salt. Cook for another 5-10 minutes until thickened. Let cool completely.

instructions.title: Prepare the Ice Base
instructions.text: Pour the 4 cups of milk into an ice cube tray or a shallow container and freeze until solid, preferably overnight. Alternatively, you can use plain water ice for a more classic approach, but milk ice offers a creamier texture.

instructions.title: Cook the Mini Rice Cakes (Tteok)
instructions.text: Bring a small pot of water to a boil. Add the mini rice cakes and cook for 2-3 minutes until they float to the surface and are chewy. Drain immediately and rinse under cold water to prevent sticking. Set aside to cool.

instructions.title: Shave the Ice
instructions.text: Once the milk ice is completely frozen, use an ice shaver to shave the ice into fine, fluffy flakes. If you do not have an ice shaver, you can use a high-powered blender to crush the ice cubes, but aim for a fine, snow-like consistency rather than coarse chunks.

instructions.title: Assemble Patbingsu Bowls
instructions.text: Divide the shaved ice evenly among four serving bowls. Form a slight mound in the center of each bowl.

instructions.title: Add Toppings and Serve
instructions.text: Generously spoon the prepared sweet red bean paste over the shaved ice in each bowl. Arrange the cooked mini rice cakes and any desired fresh fruit around the red bean paste. Drizzle with condensed milk and sprinkle with misugaru if using. Serve immediately with a spoon, allowing guests to mix the ingredients as they eat.

tips: For the creamiest texture, freeze milk in a shallow pan and scrape with a fork or use a dedicated milk ice shaver for truly fine flakes.
If you don't have misugaru, a sprinkle of finely crushed nuts like peanuts or almonds can add a similar nutty crunch.
Patbingsu is best enjoyed immediately after assembly to prevent the ice from melting too much. Serve individual portions to maintain presentation.
article: Patbingsu Red Bean Shaved Ice is a beloved Korean Desserts dish enjoyed across Korean. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.

4 scoops Ice cream (vanilla or green tea)
